opening_lines: So... who's ready to argue about something completely unimportant?
I've been saving up conversation topics all week. You're welcome.
Before anyone asks — yes, I'm fine. No, I'm not seeing anyone. Now that that's out of the way...
topic_flow: The classic catch-up: What's everyone been up to? (2-minute version, please)
Family trivia: Who can remember the most embarrassing vacation moment?
The great debate: best family recipe of all time
Upcoming plans and events to look forward to
What's one new thing you've tried recently?
questions: If our family had a reality show, what would it be called?
What's the weirdest thing you've Googled this month?
If you could swap lives with another family member for a day, who and why?
What's a skill you've secretly been trying to learn?
Who in this room would survive a zombie apocalypse the longest?

agreement: See? I always knew I was the smart one!
Finally, someone gets it. I'm framing this moment.
We should agree more often — this feels nice!
disagreement: Well, that's what makes family dinners interesting!
I respect your wrong opinion. Love you anyway!
We'll settle this the old-fashioned way — arm wrestling.
silence: Wow, I actually stumped everyone. New family record!
Is this silence agreement or are you all on your phones?
Okay, I'll answer my own question. Someone has to keep this party going.
confusion: Don't worry, I confused myself too. Let me try again.
That made more sense in my head. Version 2.0 coming up!
You know what, let's just eat instead.

engagement_strategies: Start a 'two truths and a lie' round — family edition
Create a family superlatives game (most likely to...)
Have everyone share their phone's last photo and explain it
Play 'finish the family story' where each person adds a sentence
fun_elements: Challenge: everyone does their best impression of another family member
Family bingo: 'Who said it?' with classic family quotes
Award fake trophies: 'Most Likely to Fall Asleep on the Couch'
facts: The average family dinner conversation lasts only 20 minutes
Laughing together strengthens family bonds — it releases oxytocin
Families who eat together regularly report 24% higher likelihood of eating healthy
recent_topics: Viral social media challenges to try together
New restaurants or travel spots worth checking out
transition_phrases: Speaking of embarrassing... who remembers the time...
Okay, new topic before this becomes a therapy session.
Now that we've solved that problem, let's create a new one.
poll_ideas: Hands up: who thinks grandma's cookies are better than store-bought? (Trick question)
Vote: What should we do for the next family get-together?
Quick show of hands: who's already tired? Be honest.
